hooks.command: jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty' | grep -qE '\.(js|jsx|ts|tsx)$' && echo '{"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PostToolUse", "additionalContext": "DOCUMENTATION REMINDER: This project maintains a high standard of inline documentation. For any new or modified code, ensure: (1) every exported function has a JSDoc-style block comment explaining what it does, its parameters, return value, and any important edge cases; (2) every major code section/branch has a header comment explaining the logic and the WHY behind it (not just the what); (3) any new constants or enums include a comment describing each value and its mechanical effect on the game; (4) numeric thresholds, probability values, and magic numbers are annotated with what they represent. Match the style established in gameEngine.js, agents.js, simulateHelpers.js, constants.js, teams.js, and utils.js."}}' || true
bd prime && echo '' && echo '## Available Work (bd ready)' && bd ready 2>&1 || true

hooks.prompt: STRICT GATE — default to doing nothing. Run: git diff HEAD --name-only 2>/dev/null || git status --short. From the resulting paths, IGNORE everything matching any of: README.md, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .beads/**, .claude/**, .github/**, *.json, *.lock, *.yaml, *.yml, *.md (any markdown), package.json, pnpm-lock.yaml. If zero paths remain after that filter, OR all remaining paths are outside src/ and supabase/, EXIT WITHOUT EDITING. Only proceed when actual changes under src/** or supabase/** materially alter user-visible behavior, public APIs, or the repository structure. When in doubt, do nothing — a no-op session is better than an off-topic README PR. Never edit README to document tooling, hooks, settings, agents, or issue-tracker setup. If a real source change does warrant an update, make it minimal and surgical.
